Title: FvZFP1 confers transgenic Nicotiana benthamiana resistance against plant pathogens and improves tolerance to abiotic stresses

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: FvZFP1 originates from Fragaria vesca but its function was characterized in this study using heterologous overexpression in Nicotiana benthamiana; endogenous N. benthamiana genes (NbNPR1, NbPR1, NbJAZ, NbPDF1.2, NbP5CS1, NbNCED1) were monitored only as downstream markers and were not functionally validated as core genes.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. FvZFP1 encodes a RING finger and CHY zinc finger domain-containing protein with CHY zinc finger, zinc-ribbon, and RING-finger domains, closely related to ZFPs from other Rosaceae species.
2. Stable overexpression of FvZFP1 in Nicotiana benthamiana reduces TMV accumulation and delays systemic spread, enhancing antiviral resistance.
3. FvZFP1 overexpression enhances resistance to Pst DC3000, restricting lesion development and reducing bacterial populations by up to ~20-fold relative to WT plants.
4. During pathogen infection, FvZFP1 increases ROS accumulation and activates SA signaling (upregulating NbNPR1 and NbPR1, increasing SA levels) while modulating JA signaling (altering NbJAZ and NbPDF1.2 expression and JA content).
5. FvZFP1 improves seed germination and whole-plant tolerance under salinity and drought, associated with increased SOD activity and reduced MDA content, limiting oxidative damage.
6. Under salt and drought stress, FvZFP1-overexpressing plants show upregulated ABA-related genes NbP5CS1 and NbNCED1, indicating activation of the ABA pathway in abiotic stress tolerance.
7. Overall, FvZFP1 functions as an inducer of SA and ABA pathways and ROS homeostasis, conferring broad-spectrum resistance to both biotic and abiotic stresses in transgenic plants.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression of FvZFP1 enhances resistance to TMV and Pst DC3000 and improves salinity and drought tolerance in transgenic N. benthamiana by increasing ROS accumulation during pathogen infection, elevating SOD activity and reducing MDA under salt/drought, and activating SA- and ABA-related defense gene expression.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Overexpression of FvZFP1 elevates ROS (H2O2) accumulation during early stages of TMV and Pst DC3000 infection, increases SOD activity and decreases MDA under salt and drought stress, and alters the expression of hormone-responsive genes in SA (NbNPR1, NbPR1), JA (NbJAZ, NbPDF1.2), and ABA (NbP5CS1, NbNCED1) pathways, indicating regulation of ROS homeostasis and hormone signaling to enhance stress tolerance.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Positively regulates the salicylic acid (SA) signaling pathway during TMV and Pst DC3000 infection (upregulation of NbNPR1 and NbPR1, increased SA accumulation); negatively modulates the jasmonic acid (JA) pathway during TMV infection (upregulation of negative regulator NbJAZ, downregulation of NbPDF1.2, decreased JA content), with no significant JA changes during Pst DC3000 infection; activates the abscisic acid (ABA) pathway under salinity and drought by upregulating ABA-related genes NbP5CS1 and NbNCED1.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: FvZFP1 -->(activation)--> NbNPR1 and NbPR1 expression and SA accumulation during TMV and Pst DC3000 infection; FvZFP1 -->(activation)--> NbJAZ and --| (repression)--> NbPDF1.2 expression with reduced JA levels during TMV infection; FvZFP1 -->(activation)--> NbP5CS1 and NbNCED1 expression under salinity and drought, consistent with activation of ABA signaling.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: RING finger and CHY zinc finger domain-containing protein 1 with CHY zinc finger, zinc-ribbon, and RING-finger domains; zinc finger protein (ZFP) family.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Constitutively overexpressed in transgenic Nicotiana benthamiana (35S::FvZFP1), with FvZFP1 transcript and 29 kDa protein detected in 4-week-old leaves of T3 homozygous lines but not in WT plants.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: TMV-GFP infection; Pseudomonas syringae pv. tomato DC3000 infection; salinity stress (125 mM NaCl for germination and 200 mM NaCl irrigation for seedlings); drought stress (5% PEG8000 during germination and limited watering at 5 mL/day for 14 d).
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Plant disease resistance and abiotic stress tolerance; crosstalk between SA, JA, and ABA signaling pathways mediated by a zinc finger protein.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Biotic stress (viral disease resistance to tobacco mosaic virus; bacterial disease resistance to Pseudomonas syringae pv. tomato DC3000); Abiotic stress (salt stress tolerance; drought stress tolerance).
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Upon TMV infection, TMV RNA accumulation in inoculated leaves of WT plants was ~2.5-fold higher than in FvZFP1-OE at 3 dpi and ~5-fold higher in systemic leaves at 6 dpi; SA-related genes NbNPR1 and NbPR1 were upregulated ~3-fold and ~5-fold, respectively, and SA content increased ~2-fold in FvZFP1-OE vs WT at 3 dpi; JA pathway genes NbJAZ were upregulated ~3-fold and NbPDF1.2 downregulated ~3-fold, with JA content reduced by ~27.2% in FvZFP1-OE vs WT. After Pst DC3000 infection, bacterial populations in FvZFP1-OE leaves decreased ~10-fold (2 dpi) and ~20-fold (4 dpi) relative to WT; NbNPR1 and NbPR1 were upregulated ~8-fold and ~6-fold, respectively, and SA content increased by ~49.1% in FvZFP1-OE vs WT at 6 hpi. Under 125 mM NaCl or 5% PEG8000 during germination, WT germination rates fell to 72.2% and 78.9%, whereas FvZFP1-OE remained at 92.2% and 94.4%, respectively. After 6 d of salt or drought treatment, SOD activity in FvZFP1-OE increased by ~37.3% and ~85.7%, respectively, and MDA content decreased by ~32% and ~23.6% compared to WT; ABA-pathway genes NbP5CS1 and NbNCED1 were upregulated ~2-fold under both salt and drought.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Wild-type Nicotiana benthamiana and T3 generation homozygous transgenic N. benthamiana lines overexpressing Fragaria vesca FvZFP1 (35S::FvZFP1).
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Agrobacterium-mediated transformation to generate 35S::FvZFP1 transgenic N. benthamiana; PCR, semi-quantitative RT-PCR, and Western blot to confirm FvZFP1 expression; TMV-GFP agroinfiltration and subsequent monitoring of GFP fluorescence, TMV RNA (qRT-PCR) and TMV MP-GFP protein (Western blot); Pseudomonas syringae pv. tomato DC3000 syringe infiltration and bacterial growth assays; DAB staining to visualize ROS accumulation after TMV or Pst DC3000 infection; seed germination and whole-plant assays under 125 mM NaCl or 5% PEG8000 and soil-based drought (limited irrigation) and 200 mM NaCl treatments; measurements of SOD activity and MDA content after salt/drought; HPLC-MS/MS quantification of SA and JA; qRT-PCR analysis of hormone pathway-related genes (NbNPR1, NbPR1, NbPDF1.2, NbJAZ, NbP5CS1, NbNCED1).
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable overexpression of FvZFP1 in transgenic N. benthamiana (35S::FvZFP1) combined with pathogen inoculation (TMV-GFP and Pst DC3000), abiotic stress assays (salt and drought during germination and at whole-plant stage), physiological measurements (ROS staining, SOD activity, MDA content), hormone quantification (SA and JA), and qRT-PCR analysis of hormone-responsive genes.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified from a Fragaria vesca cDNA library in a previous yeast two-hybrid screen using SVBV P6 as bait; the FvZFP1 coding sequence (804 bp) was cloned from F. vesca and used to generate 35S::FvZFP1 overexpression constructs.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Overexpression of FvZFP1 confers broad-spectrum resistance to biotic (TMV and Pst DC3000) and abiotic (salt and drought) stresses in transgenic Nicotiana benthamiana by activating SA- and ABA-related defense pathways and modulating ROS/SOD balance, indicating that FvZFP1 is a promising candidate gene to engineer multi-stress resistance in strawberry and other Rosaceae crops.
